claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit! Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin. To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your child.

**First, let me ask about your child's age:**

How old is the child you're claiming for?

- Are they **under 16** years old?
- Are they **16 to 19** years old?
- Or are they **20 or over**?

Please let me know the child's age, and if you're asking about more than one child, we can go through each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 9 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for that information. Let me work through Alex's eligibility.

Since Alex is 9 years old, he falls into the **under 16** category. Let me check the next step in the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now, the next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Since Alex is only 9 years old, I would expect he's not in paid employment, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all - he's only 9! He's in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great! Thank you for confirming. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that would be claimed in his own name. For most children, especially those as young as 9, this wouldn't apply.

Can you confirm that Alex is not receiving any benefits in his own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is only 9 years old and in primary school, I expect the answer is no, but I need to confirm. Apprenticeships typically apply to older teenagers, but certain types can affect Child Benefit eligibility depending on the region (England, Scotland, or Wales).

Can you confirm Alex is not in an apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not - he's only 9 and in primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, that makes perfect sense! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to ask about **your residency status** (as the claimant).

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if ANY of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (for up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have pre-settled status as a student with sufficient resources
7. You're a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, no - I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, let me ask some follow-up questions to see if you might still qualify under special circumstances.

**Are you living abroad in any of the following capacities?**

1. **Crown servant** - working for the UK government abroad (e.g., diplomatic service, armed forces)
2. Living in **certain qualifying countries** (specific agreements may apply)

Or alternatively:

3. Do you have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme?
4. Do you have **pre-settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme (with work, sufficient resources, or as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national)?

Could you tell me a bit more about your situation? Where are you living, and what's your employment or residency status?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm just living abroad - I'm not a Crown servant, don't have settled or pre-settled status, and it's not a special qualifying situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying your situation. Based on the information you've provided, I need to give you the assessment outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** at this time.

### Why you're not eligible:

While **Alex meets all the requirements as a qualifying child** (he's 9 years old, in primary school, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements** as the claimant.

To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, **OR**
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria such as working or having sufficient resources), **OR**
- Be a Crown servant working abroad, **OR**
- Be living in certain qualifying countries with special agreements

Since you're living abroad without any of these special circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit.

### What you could do:

- If you return to live in the UK normally, you would be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex at that time
- If your circumstances change (e.g., you become a Crown servant or obtain relevant immigration status), you may become eligible

If you have any questions about this decision or your specific circumstances change, you may want to contact HMRC directly or seek advice from a benefits advisor.

**Reference:** For more information, visit [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old, in primary school, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship - all of which would normally make him a qualifying child. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and does not qualify as a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and is not living in a qualifying country. Under UK Child Benefit rules, the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances to be eligible. Since the residency requirement is not met, the claim for Alex cannot succeed.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 9), who meets all the child-specific qualifying criteria. However, the claimant does not meet the fundamental UK residency requirements as they are living abroad without any of the special qualifying circumstances (Crown servant status, EU Settlement Scheme status, or living in certain qualifying countries). Therefore, the claim for Child Benefit cannot be approved.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
